summary: Machine policy for biometric sign-in by domain accounts. Good for stricter domain authentication baselines, but it removes biometric sign-in for domain users.

setting.name: Biometric Sign-in for Domain Accounts
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can let domain users sign in with biometrics when the device and domain setup support it. This policy controls whether domain accounts are allowed to use that biometric sign-in path.
setting.why_people_change_it: Organizations disable it when they want domain sign-in to use other methods only or want a stricter enterprise authentication policy.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling it removes biometric convenience for domain users, but it enforces a stricter domain sign-in policy.
setting.professional_notes: Biometrics.admx maps this setting to SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Biometrics\Credential Provider\Domain Accounts with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: The device does not force the domain-account biometric sign-in policy.

allowed_values.label: Domain biometric sign-in disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Domain accounts cannot use biometric sign-in through this policy path.

allowed_values.label: Domain biometric sign-in enabled
allowed_values.meaning: Domain accounts are allowed to use biometric sign-in through this policy path.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes Domain Accounts = 0 under the biometrics credential provider key, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 7 and newer systems with biometric credential provider support

states.rationale: The device policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: This policy baseline is separate from whether the domain and device actually support biometric sign-in.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Organizations that do not want to force a domain-biometrics policy
avoid_for: Environments with a strict no-biometrics rule for domain sign-in
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the environment to keep its ordinary domain biometric policy behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need to enforce that domain users cannot sign in with biometrics.
tradeoffs: Domain biometric sign-in can remain available if the environment supports it.
No explicit enterprise restriction is enforced.

recommended_profiles.label: No domain biometric sign-in
intended_for: Organizations with stricter domain sign-in rules
avoid_for: Domain users who rely on biometric sign-in convenience

states.rationale: Disable biometric sign-in for domain accounts.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want a stricter domain authentication posture.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want domain users to keep using supported biometric sign-in.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Biometrics.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file defines the domain account biometric policy and shows Domain Accounts with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Biometrics\Credential Provider.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes Domain Accounts = 0 under the biometrics credential provider policy key.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Local Microsoft policy files document the exact domain-account policy surface, value semantics, and sign-in behavior. The app writes the documented disable value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="Biometrics_EnableDomainCredProv" class="Machine" ... key="SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Biometrics\Credential Provider" valueName="Domain Accounts">; Biometrics.adml: If you disable this policy setting, Windows prevents domain users from logging on to a domain-joined computer using biometrics.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact key and value name; local ADML confirms that disabling the policy blocks biometric sign-in for domain users.
